market_positioning.short_term_view_1_5d: Keep risk tight and favor narrow commodity and energy continuation plus selective equity short exposure over broad dip buying.
market_positioning.swing_view: Swing setups are secondary and should focus on small oversold bounce watchlists until breadth and credit stabilize.
supporting_signals: Breadth was 4 advancers versus 44 decliners with average return deeply negative.
Volume and range ratios were elevated, confirming broad participation in the selloff.
Energy and commodity ETFs were the only clear positive 3 day continuation group.
Industry momentum was concentrated in oil, gas, refining, midstream, and coal.
Treasuries, aggregate bonds, investment grade, and high yield all fell together.
TLT showed the largest bond drawdown with elevated volatility, pointing to duration stress.
Credit ETFs HYG and JNK were also weak, confirming stressed risk appetite.
conflicting_signals: Commodity leadership is persistent enough to offer tactical long setups.
Several assets are statistically stretched enough to support short covering bounces.
Energy held positive 3 day and 5 day momentum despite the weak tape.

evidence: XLE had positive 3 day and 5 day returns.
USO posted strong positive 1 day and 3 day performance.
Industry leadership was concentrated in E&P, refining, integrated, and services.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: Reduce exposure if XLE loses its 3 day trend and energy leadership stops outperforming the index.

evidence: DBC and PDBC were positive across 3 day and 5 day windows.
CORN and DBA showed positive short term follow through.
Commodity ETFs were the clearest non equity leadership cluster.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: Exit if commodity ETFs lose positive 3 day momentum or leadership narrows to a single contract only.

evidence: SPY was down over 1 day, 3 day, and 5 day.
Breadth was extremely weak at 4 advancers versus 44 decliners.
Average volume and range expansion suggest the decline is broad rather than isolated.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: Cover if breadth improves materially and broad index ETFs reclaim short term trend with better participation.

evidence: XLRE and XLU were among the weakest sector ETFs on 1 day, 3 day, and 5 day views.
TLT weakness and elevated bond volatility point to ongoing rate pressure.
Real estate and utility related groups also appeared on mean reversion lists from oversold levels.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: This hedge is invalidated if long duration bonds stabilize and XLRE or XLU stop underperforming the broad market.

evidence: Silver, gold, and other precious metals ranked at the top of industry mean reversion.
Weekly losses were extreme, suggesting stretch rather than leadership.
This group is a secondary setup because the main tape remains risk off.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: Only activate size after downside pressure clearly slows, because continued weak breadth can keep oversold metals under pressure.
